source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Fire - WD 27 A Platform

actions_taken: Personnel extinguished fire with handheld fire extinguishers within six minutes
Temperature Safety Element (TSE) automatically activated Emergency Shut Down (ESD)
All nine producing wells and both compressors safely shut in
Personnel used 150 lb wheel unit and water hose to cool tower and prevent re-ignition
Compressor locked out and tagged out (LOTO)
Cooling fan tower disassembled and replaced with new unit
Gas Compressor returned to service on December 13, 2019
event.summary: On 24 November 2019 at 0700 hours, a fire occurred on West Delta 27 A Platform involving the cooler fan tower package (HAL 1161/62/63) for gas compressor unit CBA-1101. The fire was caused by ignition of escaping gas from a ruptured cooling tube in the third stage cooler (HAL-1163) when it contacted the compressor's hot exhaust system operating above 900°F. Personnel extinguished the fire within six minutes with no injuries. Equipment damage was limited to $2,040 in charred metal on the cooling tower. The facility's TSE and ESD systems functioned as designed, safely shutting down production and mitigating consequences.
recommendations: Draft safety alert addressing inspection of tubing within cooling towers
Draft safety alert addressing engine exhaust located in classified areas
Develop maintenance plan that addresses inspection of tubing integrity within cooler tower
Evaluate options for rerouting compressor exhaust out of classified areas
Implement improved cooler tubing maintenance and inspection procedures
key_phrases: long term exposure to offshore environment
no practical procedure exists to identify weak spots

hazards.name: Pressurized natural gas in cooling system
Cooler tubing degradation from offshore environment exposure

threats.name: Cooling tube rupture due to corrosion and material degradation
Gas release from ruptured cooling tube
Ignition of released gas by hot exhaust system

controls.name: Routine inspection and maintenance of cooler tubing
controls.barrier_role: Prevent loss of integrity in cooling tubes
controls.lod_basis: Preventive maintenance to detect and address tubing degradation before failure

supporting_text: BSEE found the operator's inspection and maintenance of the coolers tubing was inadequate to prevent loss of integrity, thereby allowing a gas leak
The integrity of the cooler tubes could not be fully determined during routine inspection
no practical procedure exists to identify potential weak spots in the cooler tubes

controls.name: Pressure Safety Limit (PSL) on compressor discharge
controls.barrier_role: Detect pressure drop indicating leak in cooling tower
controls.lod_basis: Automatic detection of pressure drop to trigger alarm or shutdown

supporting_text: The PSL on the compressor discharge upstream of the cooling fan tower was verified to be installed to detect leaks within the cooling tower
the leak may not have been large enough to drop the pressure downstream of the compressor below the PSL set point of 714 psi

controls.name: Exhaust system location and design
controls.barrier_role: Prevent ignition source in classified area
controls.lod_basis: Locate exhaust in unclassified area to eliminate ignition source near flammable gas

supporting_text: The compressor engine exhaust was installed in a Class 1 Division 2 area
API RP 14F states in 5.2.3 'It is recommended that engine exhausts be located in unclassified locations, whenever possible, to minimize the risk of ignition of flammable mixtures'
The compressor engine's hot exhaust system was mounted directly above the Heat Exchanger
The ignition temperature of natural gas is usually considered to be 900 °F

controls.name: Temperature Safety Element (TSE) and Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Automatically shut down production to stop gas release and reduce fire severity
controls.lod_basis: Automatic detection and response to fire/high temperature condition

supporting_text: One of the Temperature Safety Elements (TSE) on the cooling tower automatically activated the Emergency Shut Down (ESD)
All nine producing wells, along with both compressors (CBA-1101 and CBA-1102), safely shut in as designed
Several personnel also activated ESD stations in response to the event
BSEE found that the facility's TSE and ESD system worked as designed and mitigated the damage and risk of injury from the incident

controls.name: Handheld fire extinguishers and firefighting capability
controls.lod_basis: Manual firefighting response to suppress fire before escalation

supporting_text: Personnel immediately grabbed several handheld fire extinguishers and extinguished the fire within six minutes
Personnel then used a 150 lb wheel unit and water hose to keep the tower cool to ensure the fire did not reignite
BSEE found that the facility's firefighting capability was adequate to fight fires in their incipient stage
BSEE found that the personnel on the facility were well trained to respond to fire related incidents

controls.name: Personnel training on fire response procedures
controls.barrier_role: Enable rapid and effective response to fire incidents
controls.lod_basis: Training ensures personnel can execute firefighting and emergency procedures effectively

supporting_text: BSEE found that the personnel on the facility were well trained to respond to fire related incidents

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
